frameworks.name: state-space decision axis
frameworks.one_line: Bias appears as an offset along the decision axis in neural state space

frameworks.name: history-dependent choice bias
frameworks.one_line: Choices and outcomes of recent trials shape current decisions

frameworks.name: heuristic suboptimality
frameworks.one_line: Ingrained priors persist despite training and reduce gains

frameworks.name: sensory-prior integration
frameworks.one_line: Goal-directed behavior combines current sensory evidence with priors

core_question: How does prefrontal cortex represent history-dependent choice biases that shape upcoming decisions?
key_assumptions: Pre-stimulus neural state can be projected onto a decision axis
Bias is decomposable into slow and fast time-scale components
Single-trial bias is predictable from behavioral history
manipulations: Motion direction discrimination task with only current stimulus behaviorally relevant
Varied stimulus strength to probe bias effects on weak trials
Analyzed bias across slow (tens-hundreds of trials) and fast (recent trials) scales
key_findings: Highly-trained monkeys showed systematic gain-reducing choice biases
Bias fluctuated at two time scales: slow and fast (recent choices/outcomes)
Pre-stimulus prearcuate ensemble activity represented bias as a decision-axis offset
Bias offset persisted through stimulus viewing, especially affecting weak-stimulus choices
interpretations: Pre-stimulus representation of history-dependent bias is indistinguishable from upcoming choice representation
Pre-stimulus choice representation can be fully defined by biases inferred from behavioral history
connecting_signals: slow and fast bias time scales
